articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

articles.description: Borussia Dortmund captain Emre Can will miss the rest of the season with a torn ACL in his left knee.

article.headline: Dortmund snatch last Champions League spot with win over Kiel in season finale
article.description: Borussia Dortmund cruised past relegated Holstein Kiel 3-0 on Saturday to finish fourth and snatch the last Champions League spot for next season
article.linkText: Dortmund snatch last Champions League spot with win over Kiel in season finale

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Borussia Dortmund vs. Holstein Kiel - Game Highlights) 2025/05/17 ESHEET

article.story:
Borussia Dortmund cruised past relegated Holstein Kiel 3-0 on Saturday to finish fourth and snatch the last Champions League spot for next season, in the best comeback of the last seven matchdays in Bundesliga history.

Niko Kovac's Dortmund won six of their last seven matches to climb from 10th to fourth in the standings and finish on 57 points, two ahead of Freiburg who lost 3-1 at home to third-placed Eintracht Frankfurt.

Kovac had taken over in February with the team in 11th place after the departure of Nuri Sahin following a disappointing first half to the season.

No other Bundesliga team have managed such a comeback in the last seven matchdays.

"We are all happy but there is no reason to celebrate," Kovac told a press conference. "I am happy and we are all happy to have pushed this through, to have taken that last step which is sometime difficult to take."

Missing out on the lucrative Champions League would have been both a sporting and financial setback for the publicly-traded club.

"For a long time we were just passengers and on the final matchday we were in the driving seat and did it just in time," said the Croat.

"I did my job but I want to underline that you cannot achieve anything in football alone."

Dortmund players will now go into a two-week break before returning in early June ahead of the Club World Cup in the United States.

Bayern Munich had already secured the title two weeks ago. They beat Hoffenheim 4-0 while Bayer Leverkusen, last season's champions who finished runners-up, drew 2-2 at Mainz 05 to set a league record with their 34th consecutive away game without defeat.

Eintracht and Dortmund will join in the Champions League league phase while Freiburg go into the Europa League, with Mainz 05 in sixth spot qualifying for the Conference League.

With VfL Bochum and Kiel already relegated, Heidenheim finished in 16th to go into a relegation/promotion playoff.

Dortmund, needing three goals to be guaranteed a top four finish irrespective of results in the other games, got off to a dream start when Serhou Guirassy converted a third-minute penalty for the lead.

Guinea international Guirassy became the first Dortmund player to score 21 goals in his first season for the club.

Kiel went down to 10 players when Carl Johansson was sent off with a straight red card but the hosts could not make the extra man count until the break.

They did, however, score two minutes after the restart with Marcel Sabitzer slotting in from the edge of the box to make it 2-0 and Felix Nmecha adding another in the 72nd minute to seal their European spot.
